summary: PR #2744 improved the match for item function `it_80289BE8` in `itkusudama.c` by replacing an open-coded small position/velocity initialization block with the existing helper `it_80289BE8_inline(gobj, 0.1f, &pos, &vel)`. The PR body reports a targeted build and objdiff result; the bot report shows `it_80289BE8` improving from 98.08% to 99.31%.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; replaced duplicated velocity initialization statements inside `it_80289BE8` with a call to the existing inline helper.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hunk in `it_80289BE8` removes open-coded `GET_ITEM`, `vel_scale`, `pos`, and `vel` assignments and adds `it_80289BE8_inline(gobj, 0.1f, &pos, &vel);`
human_pr_text: summary says the match improves by using the existing inline helper for the small velocity initialization block

what_changed.change: Replaced a manually expanded helper-shaped block with `it_80289BE8_inline(gobj, 0.1f, &pos, &vel)`.

Removed local block contents: `Item* ip = GET_ITEM(gobj)`, `float const vel_scale = 0.1f`, position copy and y adjustment, randomized x/y velocity calculation, and zero z velocity. The following `pos.y -= 5.0f;` after the helper call remained.

smart_moves.lesson: When a decompiled block is visibly equivalent to an existing local inline helper, replacing the expanded statements with the helper can improve both source shape and match score.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: explicitly identifies use of existing inline helper for the small velocity initialization block
raw_diff: removed block was already commented as `it_80289BE8_inline(gobj, 0.1f, &pos, &vel);`
text_corpus bot report: `it_80289BE8` improved from 98.08% to 99.31%

decomp_lessons.lesson: For `itkusudama` item code, check for nearby or existing helper boundaries before retaining duplicated setup code for position and randomized velocity initialization.
scope: item functions with small repeated initialization blocks

decomp_lessons.lesson: A helper call may better represent authored source than an expanded block even when the expanded block is straightforward and typed.

naming_conventions.name: it_80289BE8_inline
naming_conventions.observation: The PR preserved and used the address-style helper name rather than inventing a semantic name; evidence only supports that it is an existing helper for the small velocity initialization block.

naming_conventions.name: gobj, pos, vel
naming_conventions.observation: The helper call uses short role names for item object, position, and velocity arguments.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Restore an existing inline/helper call instead of retaining the manually expanded body.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.result: PR body reports `objdiff-cli`: `it_80289BE8` is now 99.30514% with 34 arg mismatches; bot report shows 98.08% before and 99.31% after.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a narrow targeted build for the touched object: `ninja build/GALE01/src/melee/it/items/itkusudama.o`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.result: Reported as verification by the PR author; no full build or broader regression evidence is included in the loaded slice.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR #2744 metadata, merged state, author, timestamps, changed file counts
notes: The slice contains no review comments or reviewer discussion, so reviewer intent and review feedback are unavailable.

The PR body supplies build and objdiff verification, and the bot report independently summarizes the percentage improvement; no broader regression or adjacent-function validation is present in the loaded evidence.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use existing local inline helpers for repeated initialization blocks when they match the intended source shape
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2744, replacing an expanded position/velocity initialization block in `it_80289BE8` with existing helper `it_80289BE8_inline(gobj, 0.1f, &pos, &vel)` improved the reported objdiff score for `itkusudama` from about 98.08% to 99.31%. This is PR-specific evidence for checking helper boundaries before retaining duplicated generated-looking setup code.

source_update_candidates.title: Helper restoration can apply beyond header inlines
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2744 is an example where a local existing inline helper, not a header assert/helper, replaced an expanded block and improved matching. Curator may consider whether the `header-inlines` or authored-source-shape guidance should explicitly mention existing local inline helpers for small repeated initialization blocks.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: existing inline helper for small velocity initialization block
text_corpus issue_comment: reported improvement from 98.08% to 99.31%
source_update_candidates.reason: Current loaded standards already discuss local helper boundaries under header-inlines and authored source shape; this PR provides a concrete item-code example but is too narrow to promote automatically.

source_update_candidates.title: `itkusudama.c` has an existing helper for small velocity initialization in `it_80289BE8`
source_update_candidates.text: For `src/melee/it/items/itkusudama.c`, PR #2744 indicates that `it_80289BE8_inline` is an existing helper for a small velocity initialization block used by `it_80289BE8`; replacing the expanded block with the helper improved the match.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful file-scoped hint for future work in the same item file, but should be curated against current source before becoming a path fact.

rejection_notes: Do not infer a general rule that every small initialization block should be helperized; this PR only supports using an already-existing helper in this specific context.
Do not claim `it_80289BE8` matched fully; the reported result still had 34 arg mismatches and about 99.31% similarity.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale; no review comments are present in the loaded slice.
